content.claim: Hungary's birth cohort has collapsed from 93,000 births in 2021 to approximately 72,000-73,000 in 2025, a decline of 22% over four years, directly shrinking the child population available to sustain village-level school cohorts.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Data covers only the national aggregate; does not specify local age-cohort distribution by settlement type
Does not account for migration flows that might partially offset natural decline in specific villages
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
